message: So last spring, a surprise ice storm rolled through and I suddenly realized our 'emergency kit' was three expired granola bars, a flashlight with no batteries, and what I'm pretty sure was a bottle of water from 2019. My partner just laughed and said, "Well, at least we have snacks... kind of." We spent the whole evening scrambling—calling neighbors to borrow supplies, hunting for candles, trying to remember if we had any blankets that weren't buried in the garage. It was chaotic and honestly kind of embarrassing. The power came back after a few hours, but I kept thinking: *Why do we wait until things go sideways to get our act together?* Now we actually have the basics tucked away—fresh water, batteries, a real first-aid kit, a battery radio. Nothing fancy. Just the stuff that makes a difference when weather decides to crash the party. Nothing glamorous about being ready, but wow, does it beat the panic scramble. What's the one storm prep thing you've been meaning to tackle but keep putting off?
